meta.subject: RE-UE4SS timeline focused on the TFWWorkbench C++ ABI break on The Forever Winter (UE5.4.2)
meta.versioning: git-describe 'v3.0.1-<commits-since-tag>-g<hash>'. NO stable release since v3.0.1; the only 'latest' is the rolling experimental-latest prerelease (the unreleased v4.0.0-rc1 line). Assets are named UE4SS_v3.0.1-<N>-g<short-sha>.zip.
meta.notes: breaking=true flags a change relevant to a precompiled C++ mod (ABI) or to a runtime DataTable editor. The actual TFWWorkbench breaker is C++ ABI drift enabled by the xmake->CMake migration, NOT the FName flip (red herring). SOURCE OF RECORD CORRECTED 2026-07-15: use the 'experimental' tag (an accumulating archive of 851 builds, never deleted) -- NOT 'experimental-latest', which is rolling and wipes all assets on every build, so it can never serve a historical build. WARNING: never date a build from a DLL mtime in a redistributed bundle -- that is the repacker's timestamp. Use the UE4SS.log 'Git SHA #' banner or the UE4SS.dll SHA-256.

versions.note: Final stable release; the anchor tag for all git-describe experimental version strings.
Covers TFW's UE5.4.2. Additive. Rules out an engine-version-support gap.
Files move to <game>/ue4ss/; proxy DLL stays in Win64. Adds [Overrides] ModsFolderPaths/ControllingModsTxt (present on the local machine). Backwards-compatible; not the breaker.
Single rolling prerelease; assets force-updated continuously since. Why 'latest' is a moving target with no version bump.

BREAKING C++ change but a RED HERRING for TFWWorkbench (mod passes FNAME_Add explicitly; default not part of the mangled symbol). Verification refuted the sub-claim that it changed the Lua FName() default.
Changelog: 'cannot guarantee ABI compatability.' The root ENABLER of the C++ mod ABI instability that later bit TFWWorkbench.

versions.version: TObjectPtr<> reworked as real smart pointer (PR #850)
versions.note: BREAKING (v4.0.0-rc line; merge date unconfirmed). On the surface the mod touches via UDataTable::GetRowStruct() -> TObjectPtr<UScriptStruct>&. Candidate for a load-succeeds-then-crashes failure mode.

VERIFIED GOOD (ABI). UE4SS.dll sha256 c81d29ed..., 15,467,520 B, 3731 exports. Ships inside ConstructionVendor_easyinstall_0.9.1.3 -- which is why that mod's Nexus page states the floor 'UE4SS MUST BE v3.0.1-848 OR HIGHER'. The 'OR HIGHER' half is stale and now harmful. Resolves 81/81 imports of TFWWorkbench 0.2.1's main.dll and 83/83 of 0.1.2's.

VERIFIED GOOD, with end-to-end RUNTIME proof. narknon, 'chore: update UE submodule'; pins UEPseudo 2d1157133134116759b850b2198538923e05fb79 -- the last known pointer that still exports the int32& ?GetMinAlignment@UStruct@Unreal@RC@@QEAAAEAHXZ. UE4SS.dll sha256 678d8b02..., 15,801,856 B, 3860 exports. Ships (byte-identical to upstream) inside Modder A's 'the Codex AIO bundle'. Download: https://github.com/UE4SS-RE/RE-UE4SS/releases/download/experimental/UE4SS_v3.0.1-894-g2172883.zip (6,878,895 B).

versions.version: UVTD overhaul (2026-01-28..30) — incl. 2313a76620 'update macrosetter, membervar and vtable templates for UVTD changes'
versions.note: THE MECHANISM. A 35-commit UVTD (vtable-dumper) rework landing between good build -894 and breaking build -929. Regenerating the Unreal layer emitted VERSIONED getters (GetMinAlignment506 / GetMinAlignmentBase) and narrowed UStruct::GetMinAlignment to int16&. Not breaking on its own -- it only reaches users via the submodule bump below.

versions.version: experimental v3.0.1-929-gcd556d70 — THE FIRST BREAKING BUILD
versions.note: *** BISECTED 2026-07-15: THIS IS THE BREAK. *** narknon, 2026-01-30T23:02:24Z, 'chore: update UE submodule for FUObjectArray changes'. Touches ONLY deps/first/Unreal, bumping UEPseudo 2d115713 -> 3a756005 and importing the UVTD-narrowed UStruct::GetMinAlignment (int32& -> int16&). The old mangled export ?GetMinAlignment@UStruct@Unreal@RC@@QEAAAEAHXZ vanishes -> TFWWorkbench's main.dll fails with 0x7F. VERIFIED by binary search over the 39 archived builds in [894..1011] (5 downloads, tools/ue4ss-bisect.py): -894 GOOD / -929 BROKEN; and `compare 21728830...cd556d70` shows cd556d706a68 is the ONLY one of those 35 commits touching the submodule, so builds 895..928 are all still GOOD (never archived). GetMinAlignment appears in NO commit message -- collateral damage of an FUObjectArray refactor. NOT caused by UE 5.6 support (PR #977, 2025-08-22, is an ancestor of good build -894).

BROKEN for TFWWorkbench 0.2.1 (0x7F). Builds -998, -1000, -1005, -1008, -1009 and -1011 ALL pin the identical UEPseudo submodule b2e876da82b17254c04304746341c8fde0ddb37c, so downgrading within this range is USELESS -- they share the broken ABI. -1011-gb50986bd (2026-07-13) is what experimental-latest currently serves; -1009-gc2ac2464 is 2026-07-04. Local install DLL sha256 a79b894d... belongs to this family.
